summary: TN FF BATTLE is a private Free Fire esports tournament web application where users register with auto-generated IDs, deposit funds (₹5-₹100) via UPI ID (NIRANJAN0508@FAM), join 1v1 Clash Squad tournaments using wallet coins, and submit their FF UID + WhatsApp number. Features a comprehensive admin panel for deposit verification, coin management, tournament control, and player detail viewing. Dark Free Fire gaming theme with mobile-responsive design. Admin panel password changed to 'Niranjan@123'.
architectural_notes: Private access web application with manual link sharing
Manual admin verification workflow for deposits and coin additions
Manual room creation and WhatsApp-based room detail distribution by admin
Auto-generated unique user IDs upon registration
Real-time tournament join requests sent to admin dashboard
UPI ID-based payment flow (NIRANJAN0508@FAM) instead of QR code scanning
Real-time notification system for deposit requests in admin dashboard
Two separate password-protected admin websites: one for tournament match management, one for coin deposits
Admin panel secured with password authentication (Niranjan@123)
known_issues: 'Failed to connect the system' error occurring on wallet deposit verification page
Admin panel/section not visible in deployed application
Admin panel showing 'Access denied' message even when correct password 'admin123' is entered
'We could not connect server' error occurring on deposit page
'Actor not available' error occurring when users attempt to submit deposit requests
Deposit network connectivity issues preventing successful deposit submission

existing_features.title: Implement user authentication system with email and password registration. Upon successful registration, automatically generate and assign a unique User ID to each user. Store user credentials, email, and generated ID in the backend.

Create a wallet system for each user with coin balance tracking. Implement deposit functionality where users can enter amounts between ₹10 and ₹100. Display a Google Pay QR code when user submits a deposit request. Store pending deposit requests with amount and timestamp.

Build admin deposit verification system. Admin can view all pending deposit requests with user details and submitted amounts. Admin can approve deposits and manually add equivalent coins to user wallets using 1₹ = 1 Coin conversion rate.

Create comprehensive admin control panel with user management, tournament configuration, and match scheduling. Admin can view all registered users with their IDs, manage tournament status (Open/Closed), set tournament entry fee, set match time, and customize match schedule. Admin can view all tournament join requests with player UID and WhatsApp numbers.

Design and implement Free Fire gaming themed UI with dark background throughout the application. Use gaming-style elements including animated buttons, esports-inspired layouts, and Free Fire visual motifs. Ensure all pages are fully mobile responsive with touch-friendly controls and adaptive layouts.

Create six-page application structure: Home page (landing/welcome), Login/Register page (authentication forms), Dashboard (user overview with wallet balance and tournament status), Wallet/Deposit page (deposit form and transaction history), Tournament Page (join form and active tournaments), and Admin Panel (user management, deposit verification, tournament control).

Replace the existing Google Pay QR code displayed on the Wallet/Deposit page with the user-provided payment QR code image (Screenshot_20260219_235411.jpg). Display this QR code when a user successfully submits a deposit request between ₹10-₹100.
Add a visible website URL link or shareable website address to the application interface. The link should allow users to easily copy or share the FF PAYALUGA platform URL.
Remove the Google Pay QR code image display from the Wallet/Deposit page (WalletDeposit.tsx) after a user submits a deposit request

Display the UPI ID 'NIRANJAN0508@FAM' on the Wallet/Deposit page after a user successfully submits a deposit request between ₹10-₹100. Show clear instructions for users to complete payment using this UPI ID.

Investigate and fix the 'ACTOR NOT AVAILABLE' error occurring when users submit deposit requests on the Wallet/Deposit page. Ensure the backend actor is properly initialized and accessible during deposit submission.

Implement password-based authentication for the admin panel. Only users who enter the correct password 'admin123' can access admin controls. Add a password input screen before showing the admin dashboard.
Display tournament join request details (user UID and WhatsApp number) in the admin panel when users join tournaments. Ensure this information is clearly visible and accessible for the admin.

Add a real-time notification system in the admin panel that alerts the admin whenever a user submits a deposit request. Display notification badges or alerts showing the count of pending deposit requests.

Add tournament match management interface to the admin panel where admin can directly conduct tournament matches. Include functionality to view current matches, update match status, record results, and manage match schedules.

Add direct coin deposit functionality in the admin panel where admin can manually add coins to any user's wallet without requiring a deposit request. Include user selection interface and coin amount input with validation.

Replace current background styling across all pages with authentic Free Fire battle-themed backgrounds featuring battlefield environments, combat zones, and in-game visual elements characteristic of real Free Fire gameplay.

Update the application's color scheme and theme styling to match authentic Free Fire battle aesthetics, including Free Fire's signature color palette, UI patterns from the actual game, and combat-inspired visual elements throughout all components and pages.
Display the owner name 'NIRANJAN' prominently in the application interface, ensuring it is clearly visible and appropriately styled to match the Free Fire battle theme.
Update the HTML page title in frontend/index.html from 'FF PAYALUGA' to 'TN FF BATTLE'
Update all references to 'FF PAYALUGA' in the navigation, header sections, and branding elements across all pages to display 'TN FF BATTLE' instead
Update the URL sharing functionality in the footer to reflect the new website name 'TN FF BATTLE' in the share message or displayed text

Fix the system initialization error occurring on the deposit page (WalletDeposit.tsx) that prevents users from submitting deposit requests. Ensure the backend actor is properly initialized and available before any deposit operations are attempted.

Add proper error handling and loading states in WalletDeposit.tsx to gracefully handle actor initialization delays and failures. Display user-friendly error messages when the backend actor cannot be initialized.

Investigate and resolve the 'Failed to connect the system' error occurring on the wallet deposit page (WalletDeposit.tsx) during deposit verification. Ensure the backend actor connection is stable and properly initialized before any deposit submission attempts. Add comprehensive error logging to identify the root cause of connection failures.

Update the minimum deposit amount validation from ₹10 to ₹5 in the WalletDeposit.tsx component. Update all related UI text, validation messages, and backend validation to reflect the new minimum deposit amount of ₹5.

Enhance error handling in WalletDeposit.tsx to display user-friendly error messages when actor initialization or deposit submission fails. Replace technical error messages with clear, actionable feedback for users.

Add a visible navigation link or button to access the admin panel in the main navigation bar (Navigation.tsx). Ensure the admin link is accessible to all users who know the admin password, and is prominently displayed in the navigation menu.

Fix the admin panel access control logic to allow any user who enters the correct password 'admin123' to access the admin panel without encountering 'Access denied' messages. Ensure AdminPasswordGuard.tsx properly sets authentication state after successful password verification.

Ensure session storage keys used for admin password authentication are consistent across AdminPasswordGuard.tsx, TournamentPasswordGuard.tsx, DepositPasswordGuard.tsx, and AdminPanel.tsx to prevent authentication state mismatches.

Investigate and fix the 'loading failed' error occurring on the deposit page (WalletDeposit.tsx). Review the actor initialization logic, error handling, and loading state management to identify why the page fails to load properly. Ensure the backend actor is successfully initialized before rendering deposit form components.

Add comprehensive error logging and debugging output in WalletDeposit.tsx to capture the root cause of loading failures. Log actor initialization status, connection attempts, and any thrown errors to help diagnose the loading issue.

Simplify admin panel access to allow any user who enters the correct password 'admin123' to immediately access all admin features without additional verification or access denial messages. Remove any secondary authentication checks or role-based restrictions that may be preventing password-authenticated users from accessing the admin panel.

Review and streamline the authentication flow in AdminPanel.tsx and AdminPasswordGuard.tsx to ensure there are no redundant checks or conflicting authentication logic that could block authenticated users. Ensure the password guard component correctly sets and verifies session storage authentication state.

Diagnose and fix the 'We could not connect server' error occurring on the deposit page (WalletDeposit.tsx). Review the backend actor initialization sequence, connection state management, and error handling to identify why the server connection is failing.

Implement robust retry logic in WalletDeposit.tsx for failed backend actor connections. Add automatic retry attempts with exponential backoff when initial connection fails, and provide users with a manual retry button if automatic retries are exhausted.

Add comprehensive connection state diagnostics and error logging throughout the deposit page actor initialization flow. Log actor creation status, canister ID resolution, authentication state, and any thrown exceptions to help identify the root cause of connection failures.

Improve error messaging in WalletDeposit.tsx to replace generic 'We could not connect server' messages with specific, user-friendly error descriptions that explain the connection issue and suggest potential solutions (e.g., 'Connection timeout - please check your internet connection and try again').
Update the hardcoded admin panel password from 'admin123' to 'Niranjan@123' in AdminPasswordGuard.tsx. Ensure the password validation logic accepts the new password for authentication.
Update the hardcoded admin panel password from 'admin123' to 'Niranjan@123' in TournamentPasswordGuard.tsx to maintain consistency across all admin password-protected areas.
Update the hardcoded admin panel password from 'admin123' to 'Niranjan@123' in DepositPasswordGuard.tsx to maintain consistency across all admin password-protected areas.

Fix the 'Actor not available' error in WalletDeposit.tsx by ensuring the backend actor is fully initialized before any deposit operations. Add null checks and loading states to prevent premature access to the actor instance.

Resolve deposit network connectivity issues in WalletDeposit.tsx by improving the actor connection sequence. Ensure canister ID resolution, authentication state verification, and actor creation all complete successfully before allowing deposit submission.

Add defensive checks in the deposit submission flow to verify the actor instance exists and is ready before calling backend methods. Wrap all actor method calls in try-catch blocks with specific error handling for actor availability issues.

Enhance the useActor hook to ensure it returns a stable, initialized actor instance with proper error state management. Add validation to confirm the actor is ready before returning it to consuming components.

Add comprehensive error logging in WalletDeposit.tsx to capture the full actor initialization and deposit submission flow. Log actor creation status, canister ID resolution, authentication state, connection attempts, and all thrown exceptions to diagnose network issues.

new_feature_requests.summary: Authentication system with email/password login and auto-generated unique user IDs
Wallet system with ₹10-₹100 deposit limits, Google Pay QR code display, and coin management (1₹ = 1 Coin)
Tournament join system for 1v1 Clash Squad with FF UID and WhatsApp submission, automatic wallet deduction
Admin panel for user management, deposit verification, coin addition, tournament control, and player detail viewing
Free Fire gaming theme with dark UI, animated buttons, and mobile-responsive design
Six-page structure: Home, Login/Register, Dashboard, Wallet/Deposit, Tournament Page, Admin Panel

Implement tournament join system for Free Fire Clash Squad 1v1 mode. Users must provide their Free Fire UID and WhatsApp phone number when joining. Automatically deduct the tournament entry fee from user's wallet balance. Send user UID, phone number, and join timestamp to admin dashboard instantly.
Replace QR code payment with UPI ID display (NIRANJAN0508@FAM) for deposit transactions
Fix 'ACTOR NOT AVAILABLE' error occurring during deposit submission
Implement admin panel password protection with secret password 'admin123' - only authorized users can access admin controls
Add real-time notification system in admin panel to alert when users make deposit requests
Create backend functions to support admin tournament match management including storing match data, updating match status, and recording match results with participant information.
Create backend function for admin direct coin deposits that adds coins to user wallets without requiring user-initiated deposit requests. Include admin authorization checks and transaction logging.
Create separate standalone website for tournament match management with password protection (admin123)
Create separate standalone website for direct coin deposits to user wallets with password protection (admin123)

Create a new standalone website application specifically for tournament match management. This website must be completely separate from the main FF PAYALUGA application and should only be accessible via a distinct URL/deployment.
Implement password protection for the tournament match management website using the password 'admin123'. Users must enter this password before accessing any tournament match management features.

Include all tournament match management functionality in the new standalone website: view current matches, create new matches, update match status, record match results, manage match schedules, and search/filter matches.

Create a second standalone website application specifically for direct coin deposits to user wallets. This website must be completely separate from both the main FF PAYALUGA application and the tournament match management website, accessible via its own distinct URL/deployment.
Implement password protection for the direct coin deposit website using the password 'admin123'. Users must enter this password before accessing coin deposit functionality.

Include all direct coin deposit functionality in the new standalone website: user selection interface, coin amount input with validation, ability to manually add coins to any user's wallet, and transaction history viewing with search capabilities.

Apply the Free Fire gaming themed dark UI with gaming-style elements to both new standalone admin websites, ensuring visual consistency with the main application while maintaining their independent nature.
Update UI theme to match real Free Fire battle aesthetics with authentic game visuals and styling
Display owner/creator name 'NIRANJAN' prominently in the application interface
Fix 'Failed to connect the system' error occurring on wallet deposit page during deposit verification
Change minimum deposit amount from ₹10 to ₹5
Admin panel/section is not visible or accessible in the deployed application
Verify that the /admin route in App.tsx is properly configured and accessible. Ensure the AdminPanel page component is correctly imported and rendered when users navigate to the admin section.
Fix admin panel access control - anyone who enters the correct password 'admin123' should be granted access to the admin panel without seeing 'Access denied' messages
Review and fix the authentication flow in AdminPanel.tsx to ensure it correctly handles password-authenticated users and does not incorrectly redirect them to the AccessDeniedScreen component.

Verify that the backend canister is properly deployed and accessible. Ensure the canister ID is correctly configured in frontend environment variables and that the backend actor interface matches the deployed canister's API.

Verify that all admin panel components (AdminPanel.tsx, TournamentAdminPage.tsx, DepositAdminPage.tsx) continue to use the same session storage authentication mechanism after the password change, ensuring consistent access control across all admin features.
